qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: blackd quadratic formatting complexity allows unauthenticated DoS via deeply nested Python expressions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns blackd worker exhaustion from quadratic formatting of deeply nested expressions, with code locations in blackd request handling and formatting line generation. The advisory concerns unsanitized --python-cell-magics values being embedded in Black cache filenames, enabling arbitrary cache-file writes; the finding does not identify that option, cache filename construction, path traversal sink, or arbitrary file-write root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding matches GHSA-3936-cmfr-pm3m. The advisory root cause is arbitrary cache-file writes caused by placing attacker-controlled --python-cell-magics values into the cache filename without sanitization. The final qualifying finding instead describes a blackd denial of service caused by expensive formatting and missing executor timeouts.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-3936-cmfr-pm3m states that --python-cell-magics was placed in Black's cache filename without sanitization, allowing arbitrary file-system writes of cache files.
The session manifest identifies blackd_quadratic_formatting_dos as the only final qualifying finding.
blackd_quadratic_formatting_dos reports POST / blackd, ProcessPoolExecutor without timeout, and quadratic nested-expression formatting as the vulnerable path.
Session search found no final finding evidence for python-cell-magics, cache filename construction, the advisory patch commit, or the arbitrary cache-file write sink.
